item.title: Microcode inside the Intel 8087 floating-point chip: register exchange 摘要
item.description:
The Intel 8087 floating-point coprocessor, introduced in 1980, accelerated floating-point operations up to 100x and established the floating-point standard used today. The Opcode Collective is reverse-engineering its microcode, which consists of 1648 micro-instructions. This article focuses on the FXCH (Floating-point Exchange) instruction, which swaps the top-of-stack register with a specified stack register. Despite appearing trivial, FXCH requires 14 micro-instructions to handle empty registers, exceptions, and NaN replacement. The article details the microcode structure, register organization (stack-based with tag bits), and the internal temporary registers (tmpA, tmpB) used during execution.
标签: Intel 8087, floating-point, microcode, reverse engineering, FXCH, coprocessor, computer architecture, retro computing, hardware

item.title: Quoting Daniel Jalkut 摘要
item.description:
Daniel Jalkut offers a balanced perspective on AI, suggesting that both critics and proponents tend to be overly extreme in their views. He argues that those against AI are too opposed, while those in favor are too enthusiastic, implying a need for a more nuanced middle ground. The quote was shared by John Gruber on Daring Fireball.
标签: AI, opinion, technology, criticism, enthusiasm, balance, Daniel Jalkut, John Gruber

item.title: On first looking into JAX 摘要
item.description:
The author, an experienced PyTorch user, explores JAX and finds it impressively clean and mathematically elegant. Key differences: PyTorch is engineering-oriented, procedural, and maximalist, while JAX is math-oriented, functional, and minimalist. JAX uses JIT compilation and functional transformations like `jax.grad`, which mirrors the mathematical definition of gradients. The author illustrates this by comparing training loops: PyTorch requires explicit zero_grad, forward, backward, and step calls, whereas JAX defines a pure loss function and uses `jax.grad` to compute gradients. Despite its appeal, the author worries JAX may be doomed due to industry inertia.
标签: JAX, PyTorch, deep learning, machine learning, framework comparison, functional programming, automatic differentiation, JIT compilation, training loop, math vs engineering

item.title: I Am Retiring from Tech to Live Offline 摘要
item.description:
Chad Whitacre, a prominent figure in open source, announces his retirement from tech and online life, citing AI as the last straw. He describes his experience with Claude Code as intoxicating and unsettling, feeling a computer system sharing his inner monologue. He plans to adopt a 'Neo-Amish' lifestyle, stepping back to a pre-screen, analog existence while still using modern conveniences like cars and electricity. His departure raises concerns about open source sustainability amid AI disruption.
标签: AI, open-source, retirement, digital-detox, AI-ethics, technology-criticism, open-source-sustainability, analog-life, Claude-Code, Chad-Whitacre

item.title: Running Python ASGI apps in the browser via Pyodide + a service worker 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison describes using Claude Opus 4.8 to develop a method for running Python ASGI apps in the browser via Pyodide and Service Workers. Previously, Datasette Lite used Web Workers, which prevented JavaScript in script tags from executing. The new Service Worker approach allows full JavaScript execution, enabling Datasette plugins to work. A basic ASGI demo and a Datasette 1.0a31 demo are provided. Willison plans to upgrade Datasette Lite with this technique.
标签: python, javascript, pyodide, service-workers, asgi, datasette, datasette-lite, webassembly, claude-code, web-development

item.title: Spot checking polynomial identities 摘要
item.description:
The Schwartz-Zippel lemma states that for a non-zero polynomial P of total degree d over a finite field F, the probability that P evaluates to zero at a random point is at most d/|F|. This allows efficient verification of polynomial identities by random sampling: if two polynomials Q1 and Q2 agree at a few random points, they are almost certainly identical. Applications include testing polynomial identities, the binomial coefficient trick, and algebraic circuits used in zero-knowledge proofs. The lemma is fundamental in theoretical computer science and cryptography.
标签: Schwartz-Zippel, polynomial identity, random sampling, finite field, probability, zero-knowledge proofs, algebraic circuits, cryptography, theoretical computer science, John D. Cook

item.title: How we contain Claude across products 摘要
item.description:
Anthropic published a detailed overview of sandbox techniques used across Claude.ai, Claude Code, and Claude Cowork to constrain agent actions. Methods include process sandboxes, VMs, filesystem boundaries, and egress controls. Claude.ai uses gVisor; Claude Code uses Seatbelt (macOS) and Bubblewrap (Linux); Claude Cowork runs full VMs. The article also discusses past risks like the /v1/files exfiltration vector and highlights Anthropic's open-source Sandbox Runtime (srt) tool.
标签: sandboxing, security, AI, generative-ai, LLMs, Anthropic, Claude, Claude Code, gVisor, Bubblewrap
